notes.note:
All 15 §A items are VALID — none are already fixed in the ext-review-remediated tree. Key observations:

1. BINDING PRECISION ON A01/A02: The audit says 'Alt+H' and 'Alt+V' but the actual bindings are Cmd+Alt+H / Cmd+Alt+V due to the `if (!event.metaKey) return` guard at Hotkeys.tsx:112. This is a minor audit inaccuracy but does not change the fix — delete or wire to real behavior.

2. A06 EVIDENCE DRIFT: The audit describes a 'permanently-zero signal' badge, but the ContactsView banner is conditional on `count > 0` (line 112), so operators never see a misleading '0'. The real problem is the detection job is missing so the badge can never appear at all. The fix per decision 5 is to remove/guard the route and query rather than just hide a badge.

3. A15 LINE DRIFT: Audit cites SalesView.tsx:1122-1131 for the Export button; error-setting code is actually at lines 1047-1053 with display at 1134. The cited range still encompasses the Export button onclick wrapper (1119-1132).

4. CAP-030 COLLISION (A14) is severe: the same CAP ID is used in the canonical capability registry for Pricing Rules Chain Manager AND throughout the entire production pick/fulfillment system (dozens of references in commandBus.ts, queries.ts, sockets.ts, tests). The fix (assigning a new CAP row to release-for-picking) requires updating all those comments to the new ID.

5. HOT FILE RISK: OperationsViews.tsx (3,800+ lines) is touched by A13; Hotkeys.tsx is touched by A01/A02/A03/A07 — these four items should be serialized to avoid merge collisions. CommandPalette.tsx is touched by A12 and A13 — those two items share a collision key and should be coordinated."]

B05/B07/B09 are ALREADY_FIXED in the remediated tree. B05 proof: IdentityRibbon (src/client/components/IdentityRibbon.tsx:62) consumes goBackRouteHistory — the 'lightweight breadcrumb back button fed by routeHistory' is already shipped. B07 proof: ContactsView (src/client/views/ContactsView.tsx:112-129) has a conditional 'Review merge candidates (N)' banner gated on count > 0; the UI element fully exists. B09 proof: MobileShell header (src/client/components/mobile/MobileShell.tsx:92-104) already has a 'Desktop' link with localStorage write. UX-C06 is partially addressed — Enter-to-add exists in InventoryFinderPanel.tsx:844-846, but focus-advance-to-next-result is missing; classified VALID for the remaining gap. No §B or §C items fall under OUT_OF_SCOPE per the decisions header (which only excludes U05-U12, Q09/U11, U04 follow-ons, U13) and B09/P3 is in scope for the verify-and-add-if-missing check. Collision risk: UX-B03, B04, and C04 all touch OperatorGrid.tsx and uiStore.ts; UX-C01/C02/C03 also touch OperatorGrid.tsx — these four items should be serialized or coordinated to avoid merge conflicts on that hot file. UX-C01 (shortcuts overlay) is a prerequisite for C08 and C09 — those items share the same registry/overlay artifact. UX-B01 (Shell.tsx nav disclosure) can proceed independently.

D07 and E05 are already fixed in the remediated tree — these can be closed out immediately. D08 is P3/TRACKED_ONLY and not in scope per the decisions header. The three remaining ALREADY_FIXED items show the remediation wave made genuine progress on accessibility (aria-live channel) and KPI definitions. Key collision risks: D01+D02 both touch ToastCenter.tsx and uiStore.ts and should be batched; D03+D04 both touch OperationsViews.tsx (a known hot file per the audit) and should be serialized or carefully merged. E01+E02+E09 all touch only DashboardView.tsx and can be one PR. E04 and E09 are independent fixes in the same file and should be batched together. No items in §D or §E are OUT_OF_SCOPE — none cite U05-U12, Q09/U11, U04 follow-ons, or U13.

UX-F05 is ALREADY_FIXED — the customer money strip (balance, credit limit, open invoices count/total, last payment) is rendered at SalesView.tsx:1179-1189 using data already fetched by the customerWorkspace query. UX-G01 is ALREADY_FIXED — all three closeout mark columns are present with agSetColumnFilter and visible by default at OperationsViews.tsx:102-104.

UX-F08 is tricky: GH #352 implemented the core 'Repeat last order' functionality but placed the button in the global control band, not the customer workspace header as specified. The feature exists but the location does not match the fix description — classified VALID.

UX-F12 is a partial fix: the line grid badge and order drawer both show the below-floor reason correctly, but the internal sheet export (salesExport.ts INTERNAL_HEADERS) lacks 'belowFloorReason' — only the export part remains undone.

Collision risk: OperationsViews.tsx is touched by G01 (already fixed, no touch needed), G02, G03, G04, G05 — four §G items land in this 3,859-line hot file. The fix waves should serialize changes to OperationsViews.tsx. SalesView.tsx is the hot file for §F — F01, F02, F03, F04, F06, F07, F08, F09, F10, F12 all touch it; F11 spans SalesView+SalesSourcePane. Recommend batching SalesView changes into a single wave unit to avoid merge conflicts.

Hotkeys.tsx is only touched by F10 (add ⌥M binding). CommandPalette.tsx is touched by G03 (add applyClientCredit alias). No §F or §G items touch ToastCenter.tsx, uiStore.ts, App.tsx, commandBus.ts, MobileShell.tsx, or styles.css directly.

Note: UX-F09 is a P2 layout/architecture item — valid as described but low implementation effort and no correctness risk. UX-F11 is P2 and described as M/L effort with a note to 'schedule after F03'."

All 15 items (H01-H09, I01-I06) classify as VALID — none are already fixed, stale-evidence, out-of-scope, or tracked-only. Key collision risk: OperationsViews.tsx is touched by H01, H04, H07, H08, H09, I01, I02, I03 — 8 of 15 items land in this 3,858-line file, making it the dominant serialization bottleneck for §H+§I work. H01 (StatusActionBar for PO) and H09 (sticky header) are in the same PurchaseOrdersView function and can be co-authored without collision within the file. UX-H04 carries a backend dependency per decision 5 and should be scheduled with the backend team. UX-I01 column-count numbers in the GRID_COLUMN_AUDIT.md (2026-05-18) are now stale — Inventory has grown from 10 to 20 columns and PO from 11 to 16 — so the 'per-grid keep/hide lists' from that doc need a fresh reconciliation pass before applying. UX-H05 is partially present (arrivalStatus column renders) but lacks the editable inline control; the fix scope is adding a select/dropdown cellEditor, not a new column.

J01 is fully resolved by the backend fix at commandBus.ts:3965-3999 per Execution Decision 5; no UI-honest fallback is required. J05 is already resolved: applyEarlyPayDiscount was renamed to applyDiscount (TER-1662) and the command lives in the correct WorkspacePanel home with invoice context visible. K03 has a Trace tab wired but the intake-batch link is an explicit TODO stub — the fix requires a new tRPC query server-side. J02 is split: label flip is done (no-op), balance-effect preview is the remaining work (only change needed in QuickLedgerGrid.tsx). All K items (K01-K04) and J03, J04, J06, J07 touch OperationsViews.tsx — the 3,858-line hot file flagged by UX-T01 — making wave 4 execution prone to merge collisions if parallelized. K01 (badge cellRenderers for dueReason/scheduledFor) and K02 (confirm copy) are both in the vendorBills column definition and selectionActions sections of OperationsViews.tsx and should be sequenced or assigned to the same agent slot. J04 only touches QuickLedgerGrid.tsx and is safe to parallelize with OperationsViews work.

Cross-item observations:

1. UX-L06 is ALREADY_FIXED: the 'recalled while on line' interrupt (Scenario B) is fully implemented in PickLineScreen.tsx with alertdialog, item name, explanation, and 'Got it' / back-to-list action.

2. UX-M03 is ALREADY_FIXED: the CAP-025 inline-expand blocker drilldown shipped in Phase 5 (TER-1504). The code at OperationsViews.tsx:3018-3081 exactly matches the audit ask (count + first rows with links + View all).

3. UX-M05 depends on UX-D01/T06 (toast action API extension). The Toast type has no action support; M05 cannot be addressed without first extending uiStore.ts Toast shape and pushToast signature. Sequencing risk: M05 must wait for T06.

4. UX-L01 and UX-M01 are related: L01 adds PickView to the mobile shell; M01 adds recovery/history to PickView. Both touch PickView and App.tsx. These can collide if executed in parallel — L01 should land first as the mounting prerequisite.

5. UX-L03 presets use statuses 'in_progress' and 'needs_picking' which are not actual pick_list DB statuses (schema confirms only 'open' and 'fulfilled'). The existing FilterPresetStrip chips effectively never filter any rows. The fix needs to replace these with a correct 'Open picks' preset (status:open) plus add it as the default-active preset — FilterPresetStrip.tsx has no defaultActive capability, so that API addition is also needed.

6. UX-L04: the labelsPrinted and manifestPath columns ARE in the default fulfillment column set (OperationsViews.tsx:211-212), so the data is visible in the grid without opening the drawer. The gap is purely visual styling (boolCol 'Yes/No' text vs. chip badges). The fix is a cellRenderer customization — narrower than a full new feature.

7. OperationsViews.tsx is a hot collision file touched by L02, L03, L04, M01, M02, M04, M05 — 7 of the 11 items. All waves touching this file should serialize or be carefully branched.

Cross-item observations:

1. N01 and N02 are tightly coupled (N02 is partially blocked by N01 for the timeline-based path, though the interim 'order drawer Copy status' path is independent). They are scheduled together in Wave 5 (U01 epic). The primary collision file is ContextDrawer.tsx — any N01 implementation must coordinate with any concurrent work on that file.

2. UX-P01 is ALREADY_FIXED in the ext-review-remediated tree (column count is 8). However, the fix description contains a stale 'keep expiresAt' instruction that references a DB field that does not exist. Implementors should not add an expiresAt column without a corresponding schema migration.

3. UX-P03's field name (expiresAt) is stale — no such column exists on matchmaking tables. The concept of stale-row demoting is valid and implementable against the existing neededBy/availableDate fields with no schema change. This is a low-effort correction.

4. UX-O02 reveals PhotographyQueuePanel is entirely unused (defined but never imported). This is an orphaned component from the audit's original tree. Any O02 fix simply needs to import and mount it in the right places; no rewrite required.

5. UX-N03's evidence shows that separate directional AR/AP figures ARE already present. The 'do not net' concern is about the supplementary Net position row shown for dual-role entities, not a missing separation. The fix is narrower than the audit framing — remove or relabel the Net position row rather than restructuring the display.

6. UX-N04 is a confirmed no-op (direct SQL, no projection lag). No file changes needed.

7. UX-O05 is TRACKED_ONLY (P3) and is also effectively ALREADY_FIXED in the sense that no UI retention enforcement mentions currently exist.

8. Hot shared file collision: ContextDrawer.tsx is touched by N01 (add Timeline tab), N02 (add Copy status action), N03 (modify RelationshipContext net-position row) — three separate N-section items all modify the same file. These should be sequenced or batched carefully.

Key observations across §Q and §R:

1. UX-R06 is doubly deferred: it is P3 (tracked-only per execution decisions) AND the core behavior (desktop toggle in mobile header) is already implemented in MobileShell.tsx line 94. If the item is re-examined post-P3-deferral it should be classified ALREADY_FIXED.

2. UX-R03 straddles two states: the customer-safe gate (no cost/margin) is already met but the 'copy offer' CTA is absent pending UX-F01. Implementers should treat R03 as a conditional follow-on to F01 rather than a standalone fix.

3. UX-Q06 is the largest work unit in §Q — it bundles four distinct gaps (bulk pay command, ledger totals strip, deactivated relationship history, inline sale prompt) none of which have even been started. The bulk pay referee credits command does not exist in the catalog at all, meaning backend work is required before frontend surfacing.

4. UX-Q04 (Contacts pending-frontend commands) and UX-Q06 (referee completions) both touch files outside the hot collision set, so they are safe to execute in parallel with waves touching OperationsViews.tsx.

5. OperationsViews.tsx is touched by Q02 (via Settings tab hosting divergence panel — but CreditReviewView.tsx is the primary file), Q05 (CreditEngineSettingsPanel lives inside it), Q07 (InvoiceDisputesView lives inside it), and Q08 (SettingsView lives inside it). Four §Q items require edits to OperationsViews.tsx — high collision risk, should be serialized or split per UX-T01 decomposition.

6. FormDialog.tsx (Q03 tone/danger variant) is a shared template used by many dialogs — its test contract (FormDialog.test.tsx) must be updated alongside the implementation to avoid breaking the locked a11y tests."

Cross-item observations:

1. UX-S04 is ALREADY_FIXED for ReceiptPreviewDrawer (phase7 K5 is confirmed done: useFocusTrap at ReceiptPreviewDrawer.tsx:14). The ExpansionPanel portion cited in the audit is a non-issue for focus-return semantics (ExpansionPanel is not a dialog). Executors should close S04 rather than re-implement it.

2. UX-S01 baseline is more favorable than the audit states: the remediated tree has 10 .a11y.test.tsx files vs the audit's '~6 dialogs + Dashboard'. The Shell.sideNavAriaCurrent.a11y.test.tsx was added post-audit. However, the four specific contract gaps (StatusActionBar, InspectorDrawer, FilterPresetStrip, ToastCenter live-region) remain genuine gaps.

3. UX-T03 scope note: the audit asks to grep for 'any other pre-template *Primary* helper remnants.' salesPrimaryLabel in SalesView.tsx:1565 qualifies as such a remnant and should be swept in the same T03/H01 wave. SalesView already uses StatusActionBar for line-level (line 1241), so the pattern is established; the order-level primary just needs the same treatment.

4. UX-T01 and UX-T03 share the OperationsViews.tsx collision key. Any agent working T01 (splitting the file) will cause conflicts with any agent simultaneously touching T03 (removing purchaseOrderPrimary* from the same file). These must be serialized or coordinated.

5. UX-T06 (Toast API) and UX-T07 (Shortcuts registry) are architectural enablers with no implementation yet. T06 touches both uiStore.ts AND ToastCenter.tsx — both are hot shared files. T07 only adds a new module but Hotkeys.tsx and Shell.tsx will need updates.

6. UX-S05: the audit's concern about 'selection checkboxes are space-toggleable' is moot because OperatorGrid uses checkboxes: false. The remaining S05 gap is narrower: ExpansionPanel div+role=button pattern (K12 from phase7 partially addressed) and the tab-through-non-editable-grid limitation.

7. Phase7 audit status: K1 (ContextDrawer focus trap) is FIXED (ContextDrawer.tsx:158 uses useFocusTrap). K2 (RowCommandHistoryDrawer) is FIXED (now uses InspectorDrawer template with built-in useFocusTrap). K4 (VendorContextDrawer) is FIXED (uses InspectorDrawer template). K5 (ReceiptPreviewDrawer) is FIXED. K8 (FulfillmentView alerts drawer) is FIXED at OperationsViews.tsx:2177. K11 (SideNav aria-keyshortcuts) is NOT FIXED — directly maps to UX-S02."

UX-U02 has the highest collision risk: it touches Hotkeys.tsx, OperatorGrid.tsx, CommandPalette.tsx, uiStore.ts, and InventoryFinderPanel.tsx simultaneously — all shared with other waves. The wave should be serialized so no other unit is editing those files in parallel. UX-T07 (shortcuts registry, in scope as enabler) is a prerequisite for C01 and S02, both of which are bundled into U02; the registry module must land first before the overlay or aria-keyshortcuts sweep can execute. UX-C06 is partially done (Enter-to-add fires) — only the focus-advance-to-next-result behavior is missing; the fix is narrow (one onKeyDown handler change in InventoryFinderPanel.tsx). The RelationshipDrawer 'Copy external-safe status' button is a partial N02 delivery already in place; U01 needs to build on it with the ContextDrawer Timeline tab rather than re-implementing the copy function. UX-U04 shares MobileShell.tsx and App.tsx with any other mobile work (e.g., R02 minimal /mobile/intake from Wave 6); these two items should be batched in the same mobile wave to avoid double-touching those files.

items.evidence_found: Hotkeys.tsx:167-170: `if (event.altKey && event.key.toLowerCase() === 'h') { event.preventDefault(); pushToast('Health check requested. Watch the top status indicator.', 'info'); }`. Note: the guard at line 112 (`if (!event.metaKey) return;`) means the real binding is Cmd+Alt+H, not plain Alt+H as the audit states, but the fake-affordance defect (no real command runs, no status indicator exists) is confirmed. No top status indicator was found in the codebase.

Hotkeys.tsx:171-173: `if (event.altKey && event.key.toLowerCase() === 'v') { event.preventDefault(); pushToast('Validate All complete: visible grids are loaded from server state.', 'success'); }`. Same metaKey guard issue as A01 (actual binding is Cmd+Alt+V), but the fake-affordance defect is confirmed — no grid refetch or `verifyAllIntake` is invoked.

Hotkeys.tsx:175-182: `if (event.key === 'Enter') { ... const first = rows[0]; if (!first) return pushToast(...); if (activeView === 'sales') await runCommand('confirmSalesOrder', { orderId: first.id }, ...); if (activeView === 'orders') await runCommand('postSalesOrder', { orderId: first.id }, ...); if (activeView === 'payments') await runCommand('allocatePayment', { paymentId: first.id }, ...); }`. Operates only on `rows[0]`, ignores multi-select, has no status check, does not route through StatusActionBar decision tables. Audit cited lines 176-184; actual lines are 175-182 (one-line drift).

uiStore.ts:502-516: `partialize` function enumerates persisted keys (activeView, sideNavCollapsed, collapsedPanels, activeQuickLaunch, activeSettingsTab, drawerByView, gridColumnPrefs, dismissedShadowBanner, showMargin, lastUsedDrawerStateByView). `ledgerDrafts` is absent from this list. Execution decision 2 resolves: persist server-side per-user. The capability-registry.md:48 confirms CAP-024 promised 'Phase 3 persists ledger drafts in UI store.' — still broken.
items.corrected_scope: Per decision 2, fix is server-side per-user persistence, not adding to client-side partialize list. The fix touches uiStore.ts only to add a server-fetched draft sync, plus server-side tRPC endpoints.

items.evidence_found: OperatorGrid.tsx:662: `<EmptyState title={emptyTitle ?? 'No rows yet'}>{emptyChildren ?? 'Create or import rows, then mark them Ready when they can be posted.'}</EmptyState>`. Grep of all views for `emptyTitle` found only ~11 usages (ContactsView, SalesView x2, OperationsViews x3, MatchmakingView x3, ContactCustomerPanel). Major views with no custom emptyTitle include Payments, Vendor Payouts, Inventory, Client Ledger, Fulfillment queue, Recovery, PurchaseOrders main grid — all will render the intake-specific fallback text.

ContactsView.tsx:25: `const { data: mergeCount } = trpc.queries.mergeCandidateCount.useQuery();`. The query at queries.ts:2295 does `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM contact_merge_candidates WHERE reviewed = false AND dismissed = false`. No code in the codebase inserts rows into contact_merge_candidates outside seed.ts — searched commandBus.ts, all routers, all views for any INSERT/insert into this table and found only read/update operations. Slight evidence drift: the banner in ContactsView.tsx:112 is conditionally rendered only when `mergeCount.count > 0`, so operators won't see a persistent '0' badge — the feature is simply unreachable. The route `/contacts/merge-candidates` is still present in App.tsx:202 and accessible by direct URL.
items.corrected_scope: The fix should hide the MergeCandidatesView entry point (route guard or removal) and the badge query, not just hide a badge — the badge already auto-hides. Alternatively ship the detection job. Per decision 5 (hiding badge and deferring detection job), the fix is: add a guard to the `/contacts/merge-candidates` route and remove the mergeCandidateCount query from ContactsView.

items.evidence_found: Searched all of Hotkeys.tsx for `event.key === '/'` or any slash/filter focus handler — zero hits. Searched OperatorGrid.tsx for any keydown handler that focuses the quick-filter input — zero hits. The quick-filter input at OperatorGrid.tsx:465-471 has no `ref` that could be imperatively focused. IntakeView.tsx:45-54, 57-76: State declarations `csvOpen`, `csvText`, `csvResult`, `csvDragActive`, `csvImportFocusRef` and handlers `importCsv`, `handleCsvDrop` are present. Backend (importBatchesCsv, createBatch without purchaseOrderLineId) now rejects these flows. */}`. The CSV variables are never referenced in the JSX return. (2) src/client/components/Shell.tsx:99: `{ label: 'Receive', view: 'intake', launch: 'receiving', icon: PackagePlus, title: 'Receive product into intake' }` — Keel chip still routes to intake view with `receiving` launch mode, not a PO picker. docs/product/north-stars.md:21 also has 'Receive Inventory' vocabulary that needs updating. Execution decision 3 confirms: update work-loops/north-stars docs, re-point Receive chip to PO picker.

docs/design/spec.md §10.7 (line ~965): still lists `in_pack`, `packed`, `labeled` pick statuses that don't exist in schema. §10.8 (~974): connector status `pending` should be `open`. §10.9 (~987): command journal status `reversed` doesn't exist — reversal is expressed as `reversedByCommandId`. §10.5 (~942-947): `buyer_credit` as a payment status doesn't exist — it's a direction; real statuses are `posted | refunded | reversed`. This is a native browser `confirm()` call, bypassing the ConfirmRoot/useConfirm() pattern used elsewhere. Audit cited VendorContextDrawer.tsx:339 — exact match.

(2) src/client/App.tsx:190+194: `<Route path='connectors' element={<ConnectorsView />} />` and `<Route path='processors' element={<ProcessorsView />} />` — both still routed. OperatorGrid.tsx props at lines 77-83 define `expansionConfig?: { enabled, actionsRenderer, historyRenderer, childrenRenderer, isRowMaster }`. This supports AG Grid master-detail with ExpansionPanel as the detail renderer — but ExpansionPanel renders tabs+content, NOT a second independent grid with its own column definitions. IntakeView.tsx:2 imports `AgGridReact` directly (not OperatorGrid) and at line 350 instantiates `<AgGridReact<IntakeOrderRow>>` with `masterDetail`, `detailCellRendererParams` containing a full `detailGridOptions` with its own `columnDefs` (buildBatchColumns at line 172). OperatorGrid has no mechanism to pass `detailCellRendererParams.detailGridOptions.columnDefs` — the expansionConfig only supports the ExpansionPanel wrapper. IntakeView cannot be converged onto OperatorGrid without this extension.

uiStore.ts:62-66 defines Toast interface as `{ id, message, tone }` with no action field. uiStore.ts:157 defines pushToast signature as `(message: string, tone?: Toast['tone']) => void` — no optional action parameter. ToastCenter.tsx:26-36 renders each toast as a `<button>` with only dismiss onClick — no conditional action button. The audit's fix (extend toast shape with optional `{actionLabel, onAction}`) is not present.
